author: Mechanism
text: Companion to bl-73bb: bl-73bb covers the state-repo side (absorbed files never committed to the tracker branch); this covers the code-branch side (legacy files not git-rm'd, .gitignore not refreshed, .balls/state-repo embedded-repo hazard). The pair is the complete migration fix.

author: mark
text:
Migrate legacy committed plugin files off the code branch

bl-8a9a's first-contact path absorbs .balls/plugins/*.json into the state checkout via fs::rename but never updates the workspace's code-branch index, and ensure_main_gitignore only fires from commit_init at bl init. A repo predating bl-8a9a -- the dogfooded balls bare hub is exactly this: .balls/plugins/{github,github-issues}.json + .gitkeep tracked on main -- ends up with the plugin files showing as deleted on the code branch, a typechanged .balls/plugins symlink, and a stale .gitignore that lacks .balls/state-repo (an embedded-repo hazard on git add -A) or .balls/plugins.

Add a legacy_plugin_migrate module wired into both state_repo::ensure (cold path) and Store::ensure_state_repo's warm branch. It git rm --cached's any .balls/plugins/* paths still tracked in HEAD, refreshes .gitignore via runtime_paths::gitignore_paths (the single source of truth), and commits 'balls: migrate plugins to state checkout'. A read-only fast path -- empty legacy set + a fresh gitignore -- exits without touching the index, so the bl discover storm a parallel bl create stresses stays out of the index.lock race.

Flip .balls/plugins backstop=true in runtime_paths so bl review's add_user_changes/commit_touches_runtime block any straggling .balls/plugins/* changes on a work branch branched off the pre-migration base. Update SPEC-tracker-state.md §12 with the legacy-plugin migration row. Companion to bl-73bb (delivered on main), which fixes the state-branch side of the same migration.
